<summary>Tool use: **create_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- content:

```
# Make.com MCP Setup for VS Code GitHub Copilot

## Quick Setup

### 1. Install Make MCP Package
```bash
npm install -g make-mcp-server
```

### 2. Configure GitHub Copilot in VS Code

Add to VS Code settings or workspace settings:

**VS Code Settings (`.vscode/settings.json`)**
```json
{
  "github.copilot.mcp": {
    "servers": {
      "make": {
        "url": "https://mcp.make.com",
        "transport": "http"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**Or use the `.mcp.json` file in workspace root** (already configured)

### 3. Connect with OAuth

When you first use Make tools in GitHub Copilot:
1. A browser window will open for OAuth authentication
2. Sign in to your Make.com account
3. Select your organization (team)
4. Grant the necessary scopes
5. Authorization complete - you're ready to use Make tools

### 4. Use in GitHub Copilot

Ask questions in GitHub Copilot Chat:
- "List all my Make.com scenarios"
- "Run the [scenario name] automation"
- "Show my Make workflows"

GitHub Copilot will automatically call the Make MCP tools and provide results.

## Connection URLs

Primary: `https://mcp.make.com`
Fallback (SSE): `https://mcp.make.com/sse`
Stateless: `https://mcp.make.com/stateless`

## Troubleshooting

**Connection issues?**
- Check internet connection
- Verify Make.com account is active
- Try SSE transport: `https://mcp.make.com/sse`

**OAuth failed?**
- Clear browser cache and retry
- Make sure you select the correct organization
- Check GitHub Copilot extension is up to date

## Official Documentation
- [Make MCP Documentation](https://developers.make.com/mcp-server)
- [VS Code GitHub Copilot](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/overview)

```

- filePath: `/Users/vitaliy/Documents/GitHub/mycv/MAKE_MCP_GITHUB_COPILOT_SETUP.md`

</details></tool-use>
